data.title: Beosin: The transaction fee funds of the initial attack address launched by Bybit hackers came from Binance
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Security team Beosin said that after analyzing the address from which the Bybit hacker launched the initial attack, it was found that the handling fee funds of the address came from Binance. The corresponding 4 Binance exchange withdrawal transaction hashes are: 0x64953fc1432bf106f5e8d6b0927a39130865fec013d8403bba8fc4382515884c 0xb9f9e43dc23bdb7b231925dc01e828990d3f84b8ad3305e83ffb6848711f871c 0xd6d871deece52f15e3f2c523dffad4b85c63125d72e4de702445a654de5ce100 0x0afa81cc9c0b0bfc4a9cd46c33bcdecf58199513e7c051e5a9df1617c211f69f

data.title: Bybit CEO: Withdrawals will not be stopped, all products and services will operate as usual
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said in a live broadcast, "All products and services are running as usual. We will not stop withdrawals at this time, and we are still processing withdrawals."

data.title: Bybit Update: Bybit CEO Goes Live to Address $1.44 Billion ETH Theft Incident
data.description:
In response to the massive $1.44 billion security breach, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou has launched a live broadcast on the Bybit platform, providing further insights into the ETH asset theft that has rocked the exchange.According to previous reports, Bybit's multi-signature cold wallet was compromised, leading to unauthorized withdrawals of over 500,000 ETH-related assets, including:401,347 ETH ($1.12...
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/20617056326209?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: ByBit CEO: There Was a Problem with Multi-Signature Transfers but I Didn’t Notice it
data.description: Bybit CEO Ben Zhou revealed in a live broadcast on February 22 that a multi-signature transfer issue contributed to the $1.44 billion ETH theft. Zhou admitted that he was the last to sign the transaction using a Ledger device but failed to notice a critical problem—the delivery address was not displayed correctly during the signing process.As a result of the breach, 4,000 withdrawal applications are currently waiting for processing, highlighting the disruption to Bybit’s operations following the attack.

data.title: Bybit CEO: Bitcoin is the main reserve, other cold wallets are not affected
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bybit CEO said in a live broadcast: Bybit usually conducts internal asset transfers every three weeks, depending on the balance in our hot wallet. Whenever the hot wallet reaches the benchmark that we think needs to be replenished, a transfer transaction will occur, and I was the last signer of the transfer transaction. At that time, I checked the link, target address, code and other information, but I didn’t check it very thoroughly, which led to this theft. Later, we determined that the hacker attack and the signed code wallet were Ethereum cold wallets under the security wallet, and other cold wallets were not affected. Bitcoin is our main reserve and it is very safe.

data.title: Bybit CEO: Slow withdrawals are just a normal review process
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: In response to the theft of $1.44 billion worth of ETH from Bybit, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou said in a live broadcast that the platform has experienced large-scale withdrawals in the past two hours, and the risk and security team will review some larger amounts. This is a routine process, so please rest assured, the community.

data.title: Bybit CEO has started a live broadcast to respond to the details of the ETH theft incident
Costa Rica's Banco Nacional Introduces Bitcoin ETF
data.description:
According to CoinDesk, Banco Nacional (BN), Costa Rica's largest commercial bank, is set to launch a spot bitcoin exchange-traded fund (ETF) through its investment management division, BN Fondos. This initiative marks a significant milestone as it provides Costa Ricans with their first opportunity to invest in cryptocurrency products via the national banking system. Alongside the bitcoin ETF, BN Fondos is also introducing an S&P 500 ETF, with a minimum investment requirement of $100 for each fund. These investments will be conducted in U.S. dollars rather than Costa Rican colones.

Pablo Montes de Oca, the general manager at BN Fondos, explained that while Costa Rican regulations do not recognize bitcoin as an investment vehicle, the ETF structure allows for such investments. Banco Nacional, which serves over 2.1 million customers, representing more than 40% of Costa Rica's population, is pioneering this move in the region. Despite the absence of formal cryptocurrency laws in Costa Rica, the country's constitution and civil code permit activities not explicitly prohibited by law, allowing Costa Ricans to trade and own cryptocurrencies.

In 2022, a comprehensive crypto regulation bill known as the Crypto Asset Market Law was introduced in the Legislative Assembly. However, it stalled at the commission level. The proposed legislation aimed to formalize the use of cryptocurrencies for transactions involving goods and services without granting any cryptocurrency, including bitcoin, the status of legal tender. This development underscores the evolving landscape of cryptocurrency regulation in Costa Rica, as the nation navigates the complexities of integrating digital assets into its financial system.
